search_text: Unsupervised Foggy Scene Understanding via Self Spatial-Temporal Label Diffusion 通过自时空标签扩散的无监督雾场景理解 Understanding foggy image sequence in the driving scenes is critical for autonomous driving, but it remains a challenging task due to the difficulty in collecting and annotating real-world images of adverse weather. Recently, the self-training strategy has been considered a powerful solution for unsupervised domain adaptation, which iteratively adapts the model from the source domain to the target domain by generating target pseudo labels and re-training the model. However, the selection of confident pseudo labels inevitably suffers from the conflict between sparsity and accuracy, both of which will lead to suboptimal models. To tackle this problem, we exploit the characteristics of the foggy image sequence of driving scenes to densify the confident pseudo labels. Specifically, based on the two discoveries of local spatial similarity and adjacent temporal correspondence of the sequential image data, we propose a novel Target-Domain driven pseudo label Diffusion (TDo-Dif) scheme. It employs superpixels and optical flows to identify the spatial similarity and temporal correspondence, respectively and then diffuses the confident but sparse pseudo labels within a superpixel or a temporal corresponding pair linked by the flow. Moreover, to ensure the feature similarity of the diffused pixels, we introduce local spatial similarity loss and temporal contrastive loss in the model re-training stage. Experimental results show that our TDo-Dif scheme helps the adaptive model achieve 51.92% and 53.84% mean intersection-over-union (mIoU) on two publicly available natural foggy datasets (Foggy Zurich and Foggy Driving), which exceeds the state-of-the-art unsupervised domain adaptive semantic segmentation methods. Models and data can be found at https://github.com/velor2012/TDo-Dif. 理解驾驶场景中的雾天图像序列对于自动驾驶至关重要，但由于收集和注释恶劣天气的真实图像很困难，这仍然是一项具有挑战性的任务。最近，自训练策略被认为是无监督域适应的强大解决方案，它通过生成目标伪标签并重新训练模型，迭代地将模型从源域适应到目标域。然而，置信伪标签的选择不可避免地会遇到稀疏性和准确性之间的冲突，这两者都会导致模型不理想。为了解决这个问题，我们利用驾驶场景的雾图像序列的特征来密集化置信伪标签。具体来说，基于序列图像数据的局部空间相似性和相邻时间对应性的两个发现，我们提出了一种新颖的目标域驱动伪标签扩散（TDo-Dif）方案。它使用超像素和光流分别识别空间相似性和时间对应性，然后在超像素或由流链接的时间对应对内扩散置信但稀疏的伪标签。此外，为了确保扩散像素的特征相似性，我们在模型重新训练阶段引入了局部空间相似性损失和时间对比损失。实验结果表明，我们的 TDo-Dif 方案帮助自适应模型在两个公开的自然雾数据集（Foggy Zurich 和 Foggy Driving）上实现了 51.92% 和 53.84% 的平均交并比（mIoU），这超过了最先进的无监督域自适应语义分割方法。模型和数据可以在 https://github.com/velor2012/TDo-Dif 找到。 本文提出一种无监督的雾天场景理解方法，通过自适应时空伪标签扩散技术提升自动驾驶中雾天图像序列的语义分割性能。 Liang Liao,Wenyi Chen,Jing Xiao,Zheng Wang,Chia-Wen Lin,Shin'ichi Satoh cs.CV

authors_text: Somdyuti Paul,Andrey Norkin,Alan C. Bovik
title: Convex Hull Prediction for Adaptive Video Streaming by Recurrent Learning
title_zh: 通过循环学习实现自适应视频流的凸包预测
abstract: Adaptive video streaming relies on the construction of efficient bitrate ladders to deliver the best possible visual quality to viewers under bandwidth constraints. The traditional method of content dependent bitrate ladder selection requires a video shot to be pre-encoded with multiple encoding parameters to find the optimal operating points given by the convex hull of the resulting rate-quality curves. However, this pre-encoding step is equivalent to an exhaustive search process over the space of possible encoding parameters, which causes significant overhead in terms of both computation and time expenditure. To reduce this overhead, we propose a deep learning based method of content aware convex hull prediction. We employ a recurrent convolutional network (RCN) to implicitly analyze the spatiotemporal complexity of video shots in order to predict their convex hulls. A two-step transfer learning scheme is adopted to train our proposed RCN-Hull model, which ensures sufficient content diversity to analyze scene complexity, while also making it possible to capture the scene statistics of pristine source videos. Our experimental results reveal that our proposed model yields better approximations of the optimal convex hulls, and offers competitive time savings as compared to existing approaches. On average, the pre-encoding time was reduced by 53.8% by our method, while the average Bjontegaard delta bitrate (BD-rate) of the predicted convex hulls against ground truth was 0.26%, and the mean absolute deviation of the BD-rate distribution was 0.57%.
summary: Adaptive video streaming relies on the construction of efficient bitrate ladders to deliver the best possible visual quality to viewers under bandwidth constraints. The traditional method of content dependent bitrate ladder selection requires a video shot to be pre-encoded with multiple encoding parameters to find the optimal operating points given by the convex hull of the resulting rate-quality curves. However, this pre-encoding step is equivalent to an exhaustive search process over the space of possible encoding parameters, which causes significant overhead in terms of both computation and time expenditure. To reduce this overhead, we propose a deep learning based method of content aware convex hull prediction. We employ a recurrent convolutional network (RCN) to implicitly analyze the spatiotemporal complexity of video shots in order to predict their convex hulls. A two-step transfer learning scheme is adopted to train our proposed RCN-Hull model, which ensures sufficient content diversity to analyze scene complexity, while also making it possible to capture the scene statistics of pristine source videos. Our experimental results reveal that our proposed model yields better approximations of the optimal convex hulls, and offers competitive time savings as compared to existing approaches. On average, the pre-encoding time was reduced by 53.8% by our method, while the average Bjontegaard delta bitrate (BD-rate) of the predicted convex hulls against ground truth was 0.26%, and the mean absolute deviation of the BD-rate distribution was 0.57%.
abstract_zh: 自适应视频流依赖于高效比特率阶梯的构建，以便在带宽限制下为观众提供最佳的视觉质量。内容相关的比特率阶梯选择的传统方法需要使用多个编码参数对视频镜头进行预编码，以找到由所得速率-质量曲线的凸包给出的最佳操作点。然而，这个预编码步骤相当于对可能的编码参数空间进行详尽的搜索过程，这在计算和时间消耗方面造成了显着的开销。为了减少这种开销，我们提出了一种基于深度学习的内容感知凸包预测方法。我们采用循环卷积网络（RCN）来隐式分析视频镜头的时空复杂性，以预测其凸包。采用两步迁移学习方案来训练我们提出的 RCN-Hull 模型，这确保了足够的内容多样性来分析场景复杂性，同时也使得捕获原始源视频的场景统计成为可能。我们的实验结果表明，我们提出的模型可以更好地近似最佳凸包，并且与现有方法相比，可以节省有竞争力的时间。平均而言，我们的方法将预编码时间减少了 53.8%，而预测凸包相对于地面实况的平均 Bjontegaard delta 比特率 (BD-rate) 为 0.26%，BD-rate 分布的平均绝对偏差为 0.57%。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于循环学习的凸包预测方法，用于优化自适应视频流的比特率阶梯选择。
AI.motivation: 传统比特率阶梯预编码方法需耗时遍历所有编码参数，导致计算开销过大。
AI.method: 采用循环卷积网络（RCN）分析视频时空复杂度，并结合两阶段迁移学习训练RCN-Hull模型进行凸包预测。
AI.result: 预编码时间平均减少53.8%，预测凸包的BD-rate均值偏差为0.26%，分布绝对偏差为0.57%。
AI.conclusion: 该模型在保持预测精度的同时显著降低计算成本，为自适应视频流优化提供有效解决方案。

authors_text: Lydia R. Lucchesi,Petra M. Kuhnert,Jenny L. Davis,Lexing Xie
title: Smallset Timelines: A Visual Representation of Data Preprocessing Decisions
title_zh: 小集时间线：数据预处理决策的可视化表示
comment: In 2022 ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT '22), June 21-24, 2022, Seoul, Republic of Korea
comments: In 2022 ACM Conference on Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency (FAccT '22), June 21-24, 2022, Seoul, Republic of Korea
abstract: Data preprocessing is a crucial stage in the data analysis pipeline, with both technical and social aspects to consider. Yet, the attention it receives is often lacking in research practice and dissemination. We present the Smallset Timeline, a visualisation to help reflect on and communicate data preprocessing decisions. A "Smallset" is a small selection of rows from the original dataset containing instances of dataset alterations. The Timeline is comprised of Smallset snapshots representing different points in the preprocessing stage and captions to describe the alterations visualised at each point. Edits, additions, and deletions to the dataset are highlighted with colour. We develop the R software package, smallsets, that can create Smallset Timelines from R and Python data preprocessing scripts. Constructing the figure asks practitioners to reflect on and revise decisions as necessary, while sharing it aims to make the process accessible to a diverse range of audiences. We present two case studies to illustrate use of the Smallset Timeline for visualising preprocessing decisions. Case studies include software defect data and income survey benchmark data, in which preprocessing affects levels of data loss and group fairness in prediction tasks, respectively. We envision Smallset Timelines as a go-to data provenance tool, enabling better documentation and communication of preprocessing tasks at large.
summary: Data preprocessing is a crucial stage in the data analysis pipeline, with both technical and social aspects to consider. Case studies include software defect data and income survey benchmark data, in which preprocessing affects levels of data loss and group fairness in prediction tasks, respectively. We envision Smallset Timelines as a go-to data provenance tool, enabling better documentation and communication of preprocessing tasks at large.
abstract_zh: 数据预处理是数据分析流程中的关键阶段，需要考虑技术和社会方面。然而，它所受到的关注往往缺乏研究实践和传播。我们提出了 Smallset Timeline，这是一种可视化工具，可以帮助反思和传达数据预处理决策。 “小集”是来自原始数据集的一小部分行选择，其中包含数据集更改的实例。时间线由代表预处理阶段不同点的 Smallset 快照和描述每个点可视化更改的标题组成。对数据集的编辑、添加和删除会用颜色突出显示。我们开发了 R 软件包 Smallset，它可以从 R 和 Python 数据预处理脚本创建 Smallset 时间线。构建该图要求从业者根据需要反思和修改决策，而共享该图的目的是让不同的受众能够理解这一过程。我们提出了两个案例研究来说明如何使用 Smallset Timeline 来可视化预处理决策。案例研究包括软件缺陷数据和收入调查基准数据，其中预处理分别影响预测任务中的数据丢失水平和群体公平性。我们设想 Smallset Timelines 作为首选数据来源工具，可以更好地记录和沟通整个预处理任务。

AI.result: 通过两个案例研究，展示了Smallset Timeline在可视化预处理决策中的应用，包括软件缺陷数据和收入调查基准数据，分别展示了数据丢失和预测任务中的群体公平性问题。
AI.conclusion: Smallset Timeline被设想为一种数据溯源工具，可以更好地记录和传播大规模的数据预处理任务。

search_text: Smallset Timelines: A Visual Representation of Data Preprocessing Decisions 小集时间线：数据预处理决策的可视化表示 Data preprocessing is a crucial stage in the data analysis pipeline, with both technical and social aspects to consider. Yet, the attention it receives is often lacking in research practice and dissemination. We present the Smallset Timeline, a visualisation to help reflect on and communicate data preprocessing decisions. A "Smallset" is a small selection of rows from the original dataset containing instances of dataset alterations. The Timeline is comprised of Smallset snapshots representing different points in the preprocessing stage and captions to describe the alterations visualised at each point. Edits, additions, and deletions to the dataset are highlighted with colour. We develop the R software package, smallsets, that can create Smallset Timelines from R and Python data preprocessing scripts. Constructing the figure asks practitioners to reflect on and revise decisions as necessary, while sharing it aims to make the process accessible to a diverse range of audiences. We present two case studies to illustrate use of the Smallset Timeline for visualising preprocessing decisions. Case studies include software defect data and income survey benchmark data, in which preprocessing affects levels of data loss and group fairness in prediction tasks, respectively. We envision Smallset Timelines as a go-to data provenance tool, enabling better documentation and communication of preprocessing tasks at large. 数据预处理是数据分析流程中的关键阶段，需要考虑技术和社会方面。然而，它所受到的关注往往缺乏研究实践和传播。我们提出了 Smallset Timeline，这是一种可视化工具，可以帮助反思和传达数据预处理决策。 “小集”是来自原始数据集的一小部分行选择，其中包含数据集更改的实例。时间线由代表预处理阶段不同点的 Smallset 快照和描述每个点可视化更改的标题组成。对数据集的编辑、添加和删除会用颜色突出显示。我们开发了 R 软件包 Smallset，它可以从 R 和 Python 数据预处理脚本创建 Smallset 时间线。构建该图要求从业者根据需要反思和修改决策，而共享该图的目的是让不同的受众能够理解这一过程。我们提出了两个案例研究来说明如何使用 Smallset Timeline 来可视化预处理决策。案例研究包括软件缺陷数据和收入调查基准数据，其中预处理分别影响预测任务中的数据丢失水平和群体公平性。我们设想 Smallset Timelines 作为首选数据来源工具，可以更好地记录和沟通整个预处理任务。 本文提出了一种名为Smallset Timeline的可视化工具，用于反映和传达数据预处理过程中的决策。 Lydia R. Lucchesi,Petra M. Kuhnert,Jenny L. Davis,Lexing Xie cs.HC

authors_text: Ashkan Behzadian,Tanner Wambui Muturi,Tianjie Zhang,Hongmin Kim,Amanda Mullins,Yang Lu,Neema Jasika Owor,Yaw Adu-Gyamfi,William Buttlar,Majidifard Hamed,Armstrong Aboah,David Mensching,Spragg Robert,Matthew Corrigan,Jack Youtchef,Dave Eshan
title: The 1st Data Science for Pavements Challenge
title_zh: 第一届路面数据科学挑战赛
abstract: The Data Science for Pavement Challenge (DSPC) seeks to accelerate the research and development of automated vision systems for pavement condition monitoring and evaluation by providing a platform with benchmarked datasets and codes for teams to innovate and develop machine learning algorithms that are practice-ready for use by industry. The first edition of the competition attracted 22 teams from 8 countries. Participants were required to automatically detect and classify different types of pavement distresses present in images captured from multiple sources, and under different conditions. The competition was data-centric: teams were tasked to increase the accuracy of a predefined model architecture by utilizing various data modification methods such as cleaning, labeling and augmentation. A real-time, online evaluation system was developed to rank teams based on the F1 score. Leaderboard results showed the promise and challenges of machine for advancing automation in pavement monitoring and evaluation. This paper summarizes the solutions from the top 5 teams. These teams proposed innovations in the areas of data cleaning, annotation, augmentation, and detection parameter tuning. The F1 score for the top-ranked team was approximately 0.9. The paper concludes with a review of different experiments that worked well for the current challenge and those that did not yield any significant improvement in model accuracy.

abstract_zh: 路面数据科学挑战赛 (DSPC) 旨在通过为团队提供一个具有基准数据集和代码的平台来加速用于路面状况监测和评估的自动视觉系统的研究和开发，以创新和开发可供行业使用的实践就绪的机器学习算法。第一届比赛吸引了来自8个国家的22支队伍参赛。参与者需要自动检测和分类从多个来源、不同条件下捕获的图像中存在的不同类型的路面病害。竞赛以数据为中心：团队的任务是通过利用各种数据修改方法（例如清理、标记和增强）来提高预定义模型架构的准确性。开发了实时在线评估系统，根据 F1 分数对团队进行排名。排行榜结果显示了机器在推进路面监测和评估自动化方面的前景和挑战。本文总结了排名前 5 的团队的解决方案。这些团队在数据清理、注释、增强和检测参数调整等领域提出了创新。排名第一的球队的 F1 分数约为 0.9。本文最后回顾了对当前挑战有效的不同实验以及那些在模型准确性方面没有产生任何显着改进的实验。

AI.tldr: 本文介绍了首届路面数据科学挑战赛（DSPC）的主要任务，旨在通过提供基准数据集和代码，推动自动化视觉系统在路面状况监测与评估中的研究与开发。
AI.motivation: 通过竞赛平台促进机器学习算法的创新，加速路面病害自动检测与分类技术的实用化进程，解决传统方法效率低、成本高的问题。
AI.method: 采用数据驱动方法，要求参赛团队通过数据清洗、标注、增强及检测参数调优改进预定义模型架构，并通过实时F1分数排行榜评估模型性能。
AI.result: 冠军团队达到约0.9的F1分数，验证了自动化技术的潜力，同时揭示了复杂环境下模型准确率提升的挑战。
AI.conclusion: 总结了有效提升模型性能的实验方法，并指出当前技术瓶颈，为未来路面监测自动化研究提供了方向与改进空间。

authors_text: Siwei Chen,Xiao Ma,Zhongwen Xu
title: Imitation Learning via Differentiable Physics
title_zh: 通过可微分物理进行模仿学习
abstract: Existing imitation learning (IL) methods such as inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) usually have a double-loop training process, alternating between learning a reward function and a policy and tend to suffer long training time and high variance. In this work, we identify the benefits of differentiable physics simulators and propose a new IL method, i.e., Imitation Learning via Differentiable Physics (ILD), which gets rid of the double-loop design and achieves significant improvements in final performance, convergence speed, and stability. The proposed ILD incorporates the differentiable physics simulator as a physics prior into its computational graph for policy learning. It unrolls the dynamics by sampling actions from a parameterized policy, simply minimizing the distance between the expert trajectory and the agent trajectory, and back-propagating the gradient into the policy via temporal physics operators. With the physics prior, ILD policies can not only be transferable to unseen environment specifications but also yield higher final performance on a variety of tasks. In addition, ILD naturally forms a single-loop structure, which significantly improves the stability and training speed. To simplify the complex optimization landscape induced by temporal physics operations, ILD dynamically selects the learning objectives for each state during optimization. In our experiments, we show that ILD outperforms state-of-the-art methods in a variety of continuous control tasks with Brax, requiring only one expert demonstration. In addition, ILD can be applied to challenging deformable object manipulation tasks and can be generalized to unseen configurations.

It unrolls the dynamics by sampling actions from a parameterized policy, simply minimizing the distance between the expert trajectory and the agent trajectory, and back-propagating the gradient into the policy via temporal physics operators. With the physics prior, ILD policies can not only be transferable to unseen environment specifications but also yield higher final performance on a variety of tasks. In addition, ILD naturally forms a single-loop structure, which significantly improves the stability and training speed. To simplify the complex optimization landscape induced by temporal physics operations, ILD dynamically selects the learning objectives for each state during optimization. In our experiments, we show that ILD outperforms state-of-the-art methods in a variety of continuous control tasks with Brax, requiring only one expert demonstration. In addition, ILD can be applied to challenging deformable object manipulation tasks and can be generalized to unseen configurations.
abstract_zh: 现有的模仿学习（IL）方法，例如逆强化学习（IRL），通常具有双循环训练过程，在学习奖励函数和策略之间交替，并且往往会遭受较长的训练时间和较高的方差。在这项工作中，我们确定了可微物理模拟器的好处，并提出了一种新的 IL 方法，即通过可微物理进行模仿学习（ILD），它摆脱了双循环设计，并在最终性能、收敛速度和稳定性方面取得了显着改进。所提出的 ILD 将可微物理模拟器作为物理先验纳入其用于策略学习的计算图中。它通过从参数化策略中采样动作来展开动态，简单地最小化专家轨迹和代理轨迹之间的距离，并通过时间物理算子将梯度反向传播到策略中。凭借物理先验，ILD 策略不仅可以转移到看不见的环境规范，而​​且还可以在各种任务上产生更高的最终性能。此外，ILD自然形成单循环结构，显着提高了稳定性和训练速度。为了简化时间物理操作引起的复杂优化环境，ILD 在优化过程中动态选择每个状态的学习目标。在我们的实验中，我们证明 ILD 在 Brax 的各种连续控制任务中优于最先进的方法，只需要一次专家演示。此外，ILD 可应用于具有挑战性的可变形物体操纵任务，并可推广到看不见的配置。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于可微物理模拟器的模仿学习方法ILD，通过单循环结构提升性能与稳定性。
AI.motivation: 现有模仿学习方法存在双循环训练过程，导致训练时间长、方差高，难以有效利用物理先验知识。
AI.method: 将可微物理模拟器作为先验整合到计算图，通过单循环结构直接最小化专家轨迹与代理轨迹的距离，并利用时间物理算子反向传播梯度。
AI.result: 在Brax连续控制任务中优于现有方法，仅需一次专家演示；可处理变形物体操作任务并泛化到未见过的配置。
AI.conclusion: ILD通过物理先验和单循环设计显著提升训练效率与稳定性，适用于复杂动态环境中的模仿学习任务。

search_text: Imitation Learning via Differentiable Physics 通过可微分物理进行模仿学习 Existing imitation learning (IL) methods such as inverse reinforcement learning (IRL) usually have a double-loop training process, alternating between learning a reward function and a policy and tend to suffer long training time and high variance. In this work, we identify the benefits of differentiable physics simulators and propose a new IL method, i.e., Imitation Learning via Differentiable Physics (ILD), which gets rid of the double-loop design and achieves significant improvements in final performance, convergence speed, and stability. The proposed ILD incorporates the differentiable physics simulator as a physics prior into its computational graph for policy learning. It unrolls the dynamics by sampling actions from a parameterized policy, simply minimizing the distance between the expert trajectory and the agent trajectory, and back-propagating the gradient into the policy via temporal physics operators. With the physics prior, ILD policies can not only be transferable to unseen environment specifications but also yield higher final performance on a variety of tasks. In addition, ILD naturally forms a single-loop structure, which significantly improves the stability and training speed. To simplify the complex optimization landscape induced by temporal physics operations, ILD dynamically selects the learning objectives for each state during optimization. In our experiments, we show that ILD outperforms state-of-the-art methods in a variety of continuous control tasks with Brax, requiring only one expert demonstration. In addition, ILD can be applied to challenging deformable object manipulation tasks and can be generalized to unseen configurations. 现有的模仿学习（IL）方法，例如逆强化学习（IRL），通常具有双循环训练过程，在学习奖励函数和策略之间交替，并且往往会遭受较长的训练时间和较高的方差。在这项工作中，我们确定了可微物理模拟器的好处，并提出了一种新的 IL 方法，即通过可微物理进行模仿学习（ILD），它摆脱了双循环设计，并在最终性能、收敛速度和稳定性方面取得了显着改进。所提出的 ILD 将可微物理模拟器作为物理先验纳入其用于策略学习的计算图中。它通过从参数化策略中采样动作来展开动态，简单地最小化专家轨迹和代理轨迹之间的距离，并通过时间物理算子将梯度反向传播到策略中。凭借物理先验，ILD 策略不仅可以转移到看不见的环境规范，而​​且还可以在各种任务上产生更高的最终性能。此外，ILD自然形成单循环结构，显着提高了稳定性和训练速度。为了简化时间物理操作引起的复杂优化环境，ILD 在优化过程中动态选择每个状态的学习目标。在我们的实验中，我们证明 ILD 在 Brax 的各种连续控制任务中优于最先进的方法，只需要一次专家演示。此外，ILD 可应用于具有挑战性的可变形物体操纵任务，并可推广到看不见的配置。 本文提出一种基于可微物理模拟器的模仿学习方法ILD，通过单循环结构提升性能与稳定性。 Siwei Chen,Xiao Ma,Zhongwen Xu cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.RO

authors_text: Aman Bhatta,Vítor Albiero,Kevin W. Bowyer,Michael C. King
title: The Gender Gap in Face Recognition Accuracy Is a Hairy Problem
title_zh: 人脸识别准确率的性别差距是一个棘手的问题
abstract: It is broadly accepted that there is a "gender gap" in face recognition accuracy, with females having higher false match and false non-match rates. However, relatively little is known about the cause(s) of this gender gap. Even the recent NIST report on demographic effects lists "analyze cause and effect" under "what we did not do". We first demonstrate that female and male hairstyles have important differences that impact face recognition accuracy. In particular, compared to females, male facial hair contributes to creating a greater average difference in appearance between different male faces. We then demonstrate that when the data used to estimate recognition accuracy is balanced across gender for how hairstyles occlude the face, the initially observed gender gap in accuracy largely disappears. We show this result for two different matchers, and analyzing images of Caucasians and of African-Americans. These results suggest that future research on demographic variation in accuracy should include a check for balanced quality of the test data as part of the problem formulation. To promote reproducible research, matchers, attribute classifiers, and datasets used in this research are/will be publicly available.
summary: It is broadly accepted that there is a "gender gap" in face recognition accuracy, with females having higher false match and false non-match rates. However, relatively little is known about the cause(s) of this gender gap. Even the recent NIST report on demographic effects lists "analyze cause and effect" under "what we did not do". We first demonstrate that female and male hairstyles have important differences that impact face recognition accuracy. In particular, compared to females, male facial hair contributes to creating a greater average difference in appearance between different male faces. We then demonstrate that when the data used to estimate recognition accuracy is balanced across gender for how hairstyles occlude the face, the initially observed gender gap in accuracy largely disappears. We show this result for two different matchers, and analyzing images of Caucasians and of African-Americans. These results suggest that future research on demographic variation in accuracy should include a check for balanced quality of the test data as part of the problem formulation. To promote reproducible research, matchers, attribute classifiers, and datasets used in this research are/will be publicly available.
abstract_zh: 人们普遍认为，人脸识别准确率存在“性别差距”，女性的误匹配率和误不匹配率较高。然而，人们对这种性别差距的原因知之甚少。甚至最近的 NIST 关于人口影响的报告也在“我们没有做什么”下面列出了“分析因果关系”。我们首先证明女性和男性发型存在影响面部识别准确性的重要差异。特别是，与女性相比，男性面部毛发有助于在不同男性面孔之间产生更大的平均外观差异。然后，我们证明，当用于估计识别准确性的数据在发型如何遮挡面部的性别之间平衡时，最初观察到的准确性方面的性别差距很大程度上消失了。我们展示了两个不同匹配器的结果，并分析了白人和非裔美国人的图像。这些结果表明，未来对人口统计准确性差异的研究应包括检查测试数据的平衡质量，作为问题制定的一部分。为了促进可重复的研究，本研究中使用的匹配器、属性分类器和数据集已/将公开。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了人脸识别准确率中的性别差距是否由发型差异导致，并验证了数据平衡后的影响。
AI.motivation: 现有研究未明确性别差距的成因，NIST报告也未分析因果关系，需研究发型等外观因素对识别准确率的影响。
AI.method: 分析男女发型差异对识别的影响，平衡发型遮挡数据后重新评估准确率，使用两种匹配器及不同种族数据验证。
AI.result: 当数据平衡发型遮挡时，性别差距消失，且结果在两种匹配器及白人、非裔数据中均一致。
AI.conclusion: 未来研究需检查测试数据的平衡性，且应公开工具与数据集以促进可重复研究。

authors_text: Nasrin Kalanat,Adriana Kovashka
title: Symbolic image detection using scene and knowledge graphs
title_zh: 使用场景和知识图进行符号图像检测
abstract: Sometimes the meaning conveyed by images goes beyond the list of objects they contain; instead, images may express a powerful message to affect the viewers' minds. Inferring this message requires reasoning about the relationships between the objects, and general common-sense knowledge about the components. In this paper, we use a scene graph, a graph representation of an image, to capture visual components. In addition, we generate a knowledge graph using facts extracted from ConceptNet to reason about objects and attributes. To detect the symbols, we propose a neural network framework named SKG-Sym. The framework first generates the representations of the scene graph of the image and its knowledge graph using Graph Convolution Network. The framework then fuses the representations and uses an MLP to classify them. We extend the network further to use an attention mechanism which learn the importance of the graph representations. We evaluate our methods on a dataset of advertisements, and compare it with baseline symbolism classification methods (ResNet and VGG). Results show that our methods outperform ResNet in terms of F-score and the attention-based mechanism is competitive with VGG while it has much lower model complexity.
summary: Sometimes the meaning conveyed by images goes beyond the list of objects they contain; instead, images may express a powerful message to affect the viewers' minds. Inferring this message requires reasoning about the relationships between the objects, and general common-sense knowledge about the components. In this paper, we use a scene graph, a graph representation of an image, to capture visual components. In addition, we generate a knowledge graph using facts extracted from ConceptNet to reason about objects and attributes. To detect the symbols, we propose a neural network framework named SKG-Sym. The framework first generates the representations of the scene graph of the image and its knowledge graph using Graph Convolution Network. The framework then fuses the representations and uses an MLP to classify them. We extend the network further to use an attention mechanism which learn the importance of the graph representations. We evaluate our methods on a dataset of advertisements, and compare it with baseline symbolism classification methods (ResNet and VGG). Results show that our methods outperform ResNet in terms of F-score and the attention-based mechanism is competitive with VGG while it has much lower model complexity.
abstract_zh: 有时，图像所传达的意义超出了它们所包含的对象列表；相反，图像可以表达强有力的信息来影响观看者的思想。推断此消息需要推理对象之间的关系以及有关组件的一般常识知识。在本文中，我们使用场景图（图像的图形表示）来捕获视觉组件。此外，我们使用从 ConceptNet 中提取的事实来生成知识图来推理对象和属性。为了检测符号，我们提出了一个名为 SKG-Sym 的神经网络框架。该框架首先使用图卷积网络生成图像场景图及其知识图的表示。然后，该框架融合这些表示并使用 MLP 对它们进行分类。我们进一步扩展网络以使用注意力机制来学习图表示的重要性。我们在广告数据集上评估我们的方法，并将其与基线符号分类方法（ResNet 和 VGG）进行比较。结果表明，我们的方法在 F 分数方面优于 ResNet，并且基于注意力的机制与 VGG 具有竞争力，同时模型复杂度低得多。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合场景图和知识图谱的神经网络框架SKG-Sym，用于检测图像中的象征性符号。
AI.motivation: 传统方法难以捕捉图像中超越物体列表的象征性含义，需通过物体关系和常识知识进行推理。
AI.method: 使用图卷积网络生成场景图和知识图谱表示，融合后通过MLP分类，并引入注意力机制优化表示重要性。
AI.result: 在广告数据集上，SKG-Sym的F分数优于ResNet，注意力机制性能接近VGG但模型复杂度更低。
AI.conclusion: 结合场景与知识图谱的框架能有效提升象征性图像检测性能，且具有更高的计算效率。

ai_content.conclusion: 结合场景与知识图谱的框架能有效提升象征性图像检测性能，且具有更高的计算效率。

tldr: 本文提出了一种结合场景图和知识图谱的神经网络框架SKG-Sym，用于检测图像中的象征性符号。
motivation: 传统方法难以捕捉图像中超越物体列表的象征性含义，需通过物体关系和常识知识进行推理。
method: 使用图卷积网络生成场景图和知识图谱表示，融合后通过MLP分类，并引入注意力机制优化表示重要性。
result: 在广告数据集上，SKG-Sym的F分数优于ResNet，注意力机制性能接近VGG但模型复杂度更低。
conclusion: 结合场景与知识图谱的框架能有效提升象征性图像检测性能，且具有更高的计算效率。
search_text: Symbolic image detection using scene and knowledge graphs 使用场景和知识图进行符号图像检测 Sometimes the meaning conveyed by images goes beyond the list of objects they contain; instead, images may express a powerful message to affect the viewers' minds. Inferring this message requires reasoning about the relationships between the objects, and general common-sense knowledge about the components. In this paper, we use a scene graph, a graph representation of an image, to capture visual components. In addition, we generate a knowledge graph using facts extracted from ConceptNet to reason about objects and attributes. To detect the symbols, we propose a neural network framework named SKG-Sym. The framework first generates the representations of the scene graph of the image and its knowledge graph using Graph Convolution Network. The framework then fuses the representations and uses an MLP to classify them. We extend the network further to use an attention mechanism which learn the importance of the graph representations. We evaluate our methods on a dataset of advertisements, and compare it with baseline symbolism classification methods (ResNet and VGG). Results show that our methods outperform ResNet in terms of F-score and the attention-based mechanism is competitive with VGG while it has much lower model complexity. 有时，图像所传达的意义超出了它们所包含的对象列表；相反，图像可以表达强有力的信息来影响观看者的思想。推断此消息需要推理对象之间的关系以及有关组件的一般常识知识。在本文中，我们使用场景图（图像的图形表示）来捕获视觉组件。此外，我们使用从 ConceptNet 中提取的事实来生成知识图来推理对象和属性。为了检测符号，我们提出了一个名为 SKG-Sym 的神经网络框架。该框架首先使用图卷积网络生成图像场景图及其知识图的表示。然后，该框架融合这些表示并使用 MLP 对它们进行分类。我们进一步扩展网络以使用注意力机制来学习图表示的重要性。我们在广告数据集上评估我们的方法，并将其与基线符号分类方法（ResNet 和 VGG）进行比较。结果表明，我们的方法在 F 分数方面优于 ResNet，并且基于注意力的机制与 VGG 具有竞争力，同时模型复杂度低得多。 本文提出了一种结合场景图和知识图谱的神经网络框架SKG-Sym，用于检测图像中的象征性符号。 Nasrin Kalanat,Adriana Kovashka cs.CV,cs.LG
